steps.title: Find the constant c using function p
steps.content:
Since $p$ is linear with slope 8, we can write $p(x) = 8x + b$. Using $p(4) = 38$:
$$8(4) + b = 38$$
$$32 + b = 38$$
$$b = 6$$
So $p(x) = 8x + 6$. Now use the condition $p(c) = -2$:
$$8c + 6 = -2$$
$$8c = -8$$
$$c = -1$$

steps.title: Find the slope of t using two points
steps.content:
We now know $c = -1$, so $t(c) = t(-1) = -3$. We also know $t(5) = 51$. Using the slope formula with two points $(x_1, y_1) = (-1, -3)$ and $(x_2, y_2) = (5, 51)$:
$$m = \frac{51 - (-3)}{5 - (-1)} = \frac{54}{6} = 9$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
